title: PyPI tokens should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: PyPI API Token detected. PyPI (Python Package Index) API tokens provide secure authentication for uploading, managing, and publishing Python packages to PyPI.org or TestPyPI.org repositories. These tokens grant programmatic access to your PyPI account for package management operations, allowing package uploads, version management, project deletion, and metadata modifications. Exposing this token enables attackers to upload malicious packages under your account name, hijack existing packages by pushing trojan-infected versions, delete or yank your published packages causing service disruptions for users, modify package metadata to redirect users to malicious resources, consume your upload quota, damage your reputation as a trusted package maintainer, and compromise the Python ecosystem supply chain affecting all users who install packages from your account. Immediately revoke this token from your PyPI account settings at https://pypi.org/manage/account/token/, generate a new replacement token with minimal required scope (project-specific or read-only when possible), audit your recent package upload history and modifications for any unauthorized activity, review PyPI access logs for suspicious access patterns, consider temporarily yanking compromised package versions, notify users if malicious code was distributed, implement secure token storage using environment variables or dedicated secret management systems (never commit tokens to version control), enable two-factor authentication (2FA) on your PyPI account for additional security, use scoped tokens limited to specific projects rather than account-wide tokens, rotate tokens regularly as a security best practice, and monitor your package downloads and user feedback for signs of compromise.

PyPI API Token detected in variable assignment. PyPI (Python Package Index) API tokens are the recommended secure authentication method for Python package publishing and management operations. This token provides programmatic access to your PyPI account for uploading packages, managing project metadata, and controlling package versions. Exposing this token allows attackers to publish malicious packages under your account, hijack existing packages by uploading backdoored versions, delete legitimate package releases causing widespread disruption, modify package metadata to redirect downloads to malware, compromise the software supply chain affecting thousands of downstream users, damage your reputation as a package maintainer, and potentially gain control of critical infrastructure that depends on your packages. Immediately revoke this token from https://pypi.org/manage/account/token/, generate a new replacement token with the minimum required scope (prefer project-scoped tokens over account-wide tokens), thoroughly audit all recent package uploads and modifications in your account for unauthorized changes, review PyPI event logs and access patterns for suspicious activity, check package download statistics for unusual spikes, consider yanking compromised versions if malicious code was uploaded, notify the Python community and affected users if a supply chain compromise occurred, implement secure credential management using environment variables and secret vaults, never commit tokens to source code repositories, enable two-factor authentication on your PyPI account, use separate tokens for different CI/CD pipelines and projects, rotate tokens regularly as part of security hygiene, monitor package integrity with checksums and signatures, and implement automated security scanning in your package build and release pipeline.
